sidebar.title: Sessions
sidebar.empty: No folders yet. Click +F to create one.

bulkEdit.title: Bulk Edit — {{name}}
bulkEdit.info: Apply changes to all sessions in this folder and subfolders. Only checked fields will be updated. Jump host only applies to SSH sessions.

folderLoginSequence.title: Apply Login Sequence — {{name}}
folderLoginSequence.info: Assigns a login sequence to all sessions in this folder and subfolders.

folderCredential.title: Apply Credential Profile — {{name}}
folderCredential.info: Assigns a credential profile to all SSH sessions in this folder and subfolders. Telnet sessions are excluded.

credentialProfiles.title: Credential Manager
credentialProfiles.info: Credential profiles are shared across sessions. Each profile stores one secret in your OS keychain.
credentialProfiles.passwordHint: Leave blank to keep the existing secret.
credentialProfiles.deleteConfirm: Delete credential profile "{{name}}"? Sessions referencing it will need a new profile assigned.

loginSequences.title: Login Sequence Manager
loginSequences.info: Login sequences automate post-connection prompts (e.g. device-level username/password). Each sequence is a list of expect/send steps.
loginSequences.sendInitialCrHint: Send a CR before waiting for the first prompt. Useful for devices that need a keypress to show the login prompt.
loginSequences.deleteConfirm: Delete login sequence "{{name}}"? Sessions referencing it will need a new sequence assigned.
loginSequences.needOneStep: At least one step with a pattern is required.

session.noFolder: No folder available — create a folder first
session.legacyAlgorithmsHint: Allow negotiation of weak algorithms (group14-sha1, ssh-rsa, aes-cbc, hmac-sha1, 3des-cbc). Only enable for old systems that cannot speak modern SSH.
session.tagsJumpHostNote: Add the tag "jumphost" to make this session available as a jump host in the session properties of other sessions.
session.usernameHintKbd: Required for keyboard-interactive authentication when no credential profile is assigned.

pasteConfirm.title: Confirm paste
pasteConfirm.message: About to paste {{count}} lines into the terminal. Review the content below before continuing.
pasteConfirm.truncated: Preview truncated; the full content will be pasted.

terminal.closeTabConfirm: Close this tab and disconnect the session?
terminal.closeAllTabsConfirm: Close all {{count}} tab(s) and disconnect their sessions?

hostVerify.title: Verify Host Key
hostVerify.authenticity: The authenticity of host <1>{{host}}:{{port}}</1> cannot be established.
hostVerify.confirmConnect: Are you sure you want to continue connecting?

commandBar.freeformPlaceholder: Type a command (Enter = active, Shift+Enter = all)

settings.title: Settings
settings.closeOnDisconnectHint: When enabled, terminal tabs are automatically closed when the session ends. When disabled, the tab stays open showing the disconnected state.
settings.openLocalOnStartupHint: When enabled, a local terminal tab is automatically opened when the application starts. When disabled, the application starts with no open tabs.
settings.confirmOnCloseTabHint: When enabled, a confirmation dialog is shown before closing a terminal tab. When disabled, tabs close immediately.
settings.confirmOnQuitLabel: Confirm before quitting with open sessions
settings.autoRefreshHint: Periodically reload folders and sessions from the database. Useful when multiple clients share a PostgreSQL backend. Set to Off to disable.
settings.connectTimeoutHint: Maximum time to wait when establishing an SSH or Telnet connection before giving up. Increase this if you connect to hosts on slow or high-latency networks.
settings.keepaliveIntervalHint: How often to send an SSH-level keepalive message to each connected host. Lower values prevent idle disconnects on firewalls with short timeouts. Set to Off to disable keepalives entirely.
settings.keepaliveMaxHint: Number of missed keepalive replies before the session is considered dead and disconnected.
settings.toastAutoDismissHint: When enabled, toast notifications are automatically dismissed after a set number of seconds. When disabled, notifications stay until manually closed.
settings.confirmOnQuitHint: When enabled, a confirmation dialog is shown before quitting if there are open terminal tabs. When disabled, the application closes immediately on Ctrl+Q / Cmd+Q regardless of open sessions.
settings.quitConfirmMessage: There are {{count}} open session(s). Are you sure you want to quit?
settings.fontSizeHint: Use Ctrl+Plus / Ctrl+Minus in an active terminal to zoom in/out. Ctrl+0 resets to default (14).
settings.copyOnSelectHint: When enabled, selected text is automatically copied to the clipboard (PuTTY-style). When disabled, use Ctrl+Shift+C to copy.
settings.pasteOnRightClickHint: When enabled, right-clicking in the terminal pastes clipboard content (PuTTY-style).
settings.confirmMultilinePasteHint: When enabled, a preview dialog appears whenever pasted clipboard content contains line breaks or escape sequences. Helps prevent accidentally executing commands hidden in copied text ("paste-jacking").
settings.dbSqlitePathHint: Path to the SQLite database file. Leave empty to use the default location. Changing this requires a restart.
settings.dbSslModeDisableWarning: Warning: with SSL disabled, your database username and password are sent in plaintext to the server.
settings.dbSslModePreferWarning: Note: 'Prefer' falls back to plaintext if the server does not support TLS. Use 'Require' for guaranteed encryption.
settings.dbPasswordUnchangedHint: Leave empty to keep the currently saved password.
settings.dbTestDbNotFound: Server reachable and credentials valid, but the database does not exist yet. Use "Create Database" to create it.
settings.dbRestartRequired: Application restart required for changes to take effect.
settings.dbCredentialNote: Credentials are always stored locally per user, never in the central database.
settings.pgIsolationNote: Session isolation requires individual PostgreSQL accounts. With a shared database account, all sessions are visible to all users.
settings.userIdentHint: Used to map your credential profiles to sessions across machines. Must be unique per user.
settings.dbExportImportHint: Export and import ShellStation session data. To import from external tools, use the section below.
settings.dbFallbackBanner: Could not connect to PostgreSQL: {{message}}. Using offline mode.
settings.restrictPrivateIpsHint: When enabled, SSH connections to loopback (127.x), link-local, and private RFC 1918 addresses (10.x, 172.16-31.x, 192.168.x) are blocked. Disable this if you connect to hosts on your local network.
settings.importTooLarge: Import file is too large (max 100 MB).
settings.loggingEnabledHint: When enabled, terminal output is written to log files for each session.
settings.loggingDirectoryHint: Directory where session log files will be stored.
settings.loggingFilenameHint: Placeholders: {name} = session name, {mm} = minute, {hh} = hour, {dd} = day, {MM} = month, {yy} = year
settings.appLoggingEnabledHint: When enabled, ShellStation writes its own diagnostic logs (startup, database, SSH, errors) to a daily-rotating file. Restart the application for changes to take effect.
settings.appLoggingDirectoryHint: Directory where application log files will be stored. Files are rotated daily as shellstation.YYYY-MM-DD.log.
settings.appLoggingLevelHint: Verbosity of application logs. INFO is recommended; DEBUG and TRACE are useful when diagnosing problems. The RUST_LOG environment variable, if set, overrides this value.
settings.appLoggingSaved: Application logging settings saved. Restart the app for changes to take effect.

highlighting.sectionHint: Define regex-based highlighting rules for terminal output and assign profiles to sessions.

userIdent.promptMessage: Your user identity is used to map credential profiles to sessions. This must be unique per person and consistent across all your machines.
